title: Shared run read model
goal: Introduce a shared daemon-owned RunReadModel/read-model builder so status, terminal run summaries, and handoff/report paths consume the same authoritative run snapshot instead of hand-assembling parallel views of run truth.
acceptance: All existing projection snapshots, parity fixtures, and golden output tests remain behavior-preserving unless an intentional divergence is explicitly documented in this slice output.
A single read-model builder constructs RunDetails/feed inputs for daemon status/latest status, terminal run-summary generation, and handoff/report readiness paths that need run truth.

write_terminal_run_summary no longer hand-builds a partial RunDetails with empty incidents, default replan status, or missing primary_terminal_reason when the shared read model can supply those fields.

The read model supports explicit terminal status/error overrides for pre-advertisement summaries without diverging from live status semantics for incidents, questions, replan, economics, profile, plan revisions, and feed projection.
Status JSON and run-summary.json agree on terminal reason/feed/operator command semantics for blocked/failed/completed/cancelled transitions in covered tests.
The read-model interface is deep: callers request a run snapshot with clear options rather than coordinating Store/Event/Artifact/Profile/Projection details themselves.
must_ask_if: The implementation would change public status JSON fields incompatibly rather than adding or preserving fields.
Terminal summaries would stop being written before terminal notification delivery.
The implementation would make handoff depend on Herdr, pane text, or monitor output.
Existing tests or snapshots would need broad wording changes unrelated to eliminating read-model drift.
